name: Texas Medical Center — West Pavilion
explanation: Large medical complex with high cooling demand, significant roof area for capture, and strong ESG commitment. Houston's high rainfall and flood risk amplify the resilience value.
visual_notes: Multiple cooling towers visible on west wing rooftop. Large flat roof sections suitable for collection.

name: Lone Star Industrial Park — Building A
explanation: Massive industrial roof in a water-stressed area. While cooling tower presence is uncertain, the sheer capture potential and high water stress make this a strong rainfall-driven candidate.
visual_notes: Large flat-roof warehouse. No obvious cooling structures. Potential for large-scale collection system.

name: Georgia-Pacific Plaza
explanation: High-confidence cooling tower presence in a high-rainfall city. Strong LEED and ENERGY STAR ratings indicate adoption readiness. ESG commitment supports investment case.
visual_notes: Cooling towers confirmed on roof. LEED Gold certified building. Modern HVAC infrastructure visible.

name: Phoenix Semiconductor Fab
explanation: Extreme cooling demand in arid climate with massive roof. Very high water stress amplifies value of every captured gallon. Low rainfall limits capture volume but high water costs and stress make even small volumes valuable.
visual_notes: Multiple large cooling tower banks visible. Massive flat roof. Industrial clean-room facility.

name: Miami Convention Center
explanation: Extremely high flood risk makes stormwater capture a resilience imperative. High rainfall volume with large roof area creates significant capture potential. LEED certification shows sustainability commitment.
visual_notes: Large convention roof area. Cooling infrastructure partially visible. Flood zone location confirmed.

name: Raleigh Data Center Campus
explanation: Data centers have extreme cooling needs. Very high confidence cooling tower detection with strong ESG and LEED credentials. Excellent roof capture potential with moderate rainfall.
visual_notes: Extensive cooling tower array confirmed. Multiple large flat-roof structures. Modern sustainable design elements.

name: Denver Federal Center — Building 67
explanation: Colorado's strong rainwater policy framework and high water stress create a favorable policy environment. Moderate across all dimensions. No single dominant driver but balanced viability.
visual_notes: Federal building with standard commercial roof. No prominent cooling structures. Well-maintained facility.

name: Gulf Coast Refinery Support Complex
explanation: Massive roof area in a high-rainfall, high-flood-risk zone. Industrial facility with significant cooling needs. Resilience and stormwater management are primary value drivers despite weak policy environment.
visual_notes: Industrial complex with large flat roofs. Cooling towers visible near processing areas. Flood-prone area.

name: Austin Tech Campus — Main Building
explanation: Well-rounded candidate with strong scores across all categories. Austin's progressive water policy, growing tech sector ESG commitment, and moderate climate create a balanced opportunity.
visual_notes: Modern office campus with cooling infrastructure. LEED Platinum certified. Solar panels also visible on portions of roof.

name: Carolina Textile Mill — Warehouse District
explanation: Large industrial roof with good rainfall creates strong capture potential. Moderate across other dimensions. Lack of sustainability certifications may indicate less adoption readiness but large physical opportunity.
visual_notes: Historic industrial building with expansive flat roof. Some HVAC equipment visible but no confirmed cooling towers.
